description: GUI app (Python + NiceGUI) for managing Claude Code skills — clone repos, detect skills, symlink to personal and project targets

userStories.title: Project scaffold and launchable app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the package structure and entrypoint in place so that `uv run skillmanager` launches the NiceGUI app.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skillmanager/__init__.py exists
src/skillmanager/main.py exists with a run() function that starts NiceGUI on localhost only
pyproject.toml has [project.scripts] entry: skillmanager = 'skillmanager.main:run'
pyproject.toml has [build-system] using hatchling with packages = ['src/skillmanager']
App title is 'Skill Manager'
`uv run skillmanager` launches the app without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: pyproject.toml already updated with scripts and build-system sections. src/skillmanager/ directory already created. Just needs __init__.py, main.py, and verify launch works.

userStories.title: Config and data models layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need XDG-compliant config paths and typed dataclasses so all other modules have a stable data layer to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: src/skillmanager/models.py defines dataclasses: Skill (name, rel_path, enabled), Source (id, display_name, kind, path, url, skills, confirmed, last_updated), Project (id, display_name, path), SymlinkTarget, ConflictResolution
Source.kind is an enum with REMOTE and LOCAL values
src/skillmanager/config.py defines CONFIG_DIR = ~/.config/skillmanager/, REPOS_DIR = ~/.local/share/skillmanager/repos/
config.py has load_config() -> AppConfig and save_config(AppConfig) functions
AppConfig holds lists of Source and Project objects
Config is persisted as TOML using tomllib (read) and tomli-w (write)
Missing config file returns empty AppConfig (no error)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Python 3.11 stdlib tomllib for reading. tomli-w is already in dependencies for writing.

userStories.title: Sidebar + detail panel layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a sidebar with Skill Sources and Projects sections and a detail panel on the right so I can navigate the app.
acceptanceCriteria: App uses ui.splitter with sidebar (~250px) on left and detail panel on right
Sidebar has two collapsible sections: 'Skill Sources' and 'Projects'
Each section has an 'Add' button (wired to placeholder dialogs for now)
Clicking a sidebar entry highlights it and updates the detail panel
Detail panel shows placeholder text when nothing is selected
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Sidebar entries are populated from AppConfig loaded at startup. Use ui.splitter, ui.expansion for collapsible sections.

userStories.title: Add source dialog — remote URL and local path
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add a skill repository by pasting a remote git URL or providing a local path so the app can track it.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Add' button in Skill Sources section opens a dialog
Dialog has a toggle between 'Remote URL' and 'Local Path' modes
Remote mode: text input for git URL, optional display name input
Local mode: text input for directory path, optional display name input
Display name defaults to the last path segment of the URL or path
Clicking Cancel closes dialog without saving
Clicking Add triggers validation (see US-005 for git clone, US-002 for path check)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Dialog only — git clone and path validation happen in US-005. This story is purely the UI shell.

userStories.title: Clone remote repo and register local path
userStories.description: As a user, when I submit the Add Source dialog, I want the app to clone the remote repo or validate and register the local path, then show the new source in the sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Remote URL: app runs `git clone <url> <repos_dir>/<slug>` using subprocess and system git
Clone progress/errors are shown inline in the dialog
Local path: app checks path exists; shows inline error if not
On success: Source is added to AppConfig, saved to TOML, appears in sidebar
Sidebar entry is auto-selected and detail panel opens for the new source
Duplicate URL or path shows an inline error without closing dialog
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', url, str(dest)], capture_output=True). REPOS_DIR is ~/.local/share/skillmanager/repos/.

userStories.title: Auto-detect skills and confirmation UI
userStories.description: As a user, after adding a source, I want the detail panel to show candidate skill folders so I can confirm or exclude them before symlinking.
acceptanceCriteria: Source detail panel shows a list of candidate skill folders (subdirectories containing at least one .md file, repo root excluded)
Each candidate has a checkbox defaulting to checked
Folders named 'docs', 'tests', '.github', 'flowchart' are detected but default to unchecked
'Confirm Skills' button saves the checked selection to Source.skills and sets Source.confirmed = True
After confirming, the detail panel shows the confirmed skill list (not the detection UI)
Unconfirmed sources show a banner: 'Confirm skills to enable symlinking'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Walk source.path subdirectories. A candidate is any dir with at least one .md file at any depth. Use os.walk or pathlib.rglob('*.md').

userStories.title: Add managed project
userStories.description: As a user, I want to register a project directory so I can symlink skills into its .claude/skills/ folder.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Add' button in Projects sidebar section opens a dialog
Dialog has a path input and a display name input (defaults to folder name)
Inline error shown if path does not exist on disk
On success: Project saved to AppConfig, appears in sidebar
App auto-creates <project_path>/.claude/skills/ if it does not exist
New project is auto-selected in sidebar and detail panel opens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Project.skills_dir property returns path / '.claude' / 'skills'. Create it with Path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True).

userStories.title: Source and project detail panels
userStories.description: As a user, I want clicking a source or project in the sidebar to show a detail panel with its metadata and action buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Source detail panel shows: display name, kind (Remote/Local), path/URL, confirmed skills list, last-updated timestamp
Source detail panel has 'Update' button (remote only, disabled for local with tooltip) and 'Remove' button
Project detail panel shows: display name, path, .claude/skills/ path, symlink count summary
Project detail panel has 'Edit Name' inline and 'Remove' button
Buttons are wired to placeholder handlers (actual logic in later stories)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Keep handlers as stubs (ui.notify('Coming soon')) for Update, Remove, Edit. Full logic in US-011, US-013, US-014.

userStories.title: Symlink checkbox matrix — layout and read state
userStories.description: As a user, I want a matrix view showing all confirmed skills as rows and all targets (Personal + projects) as columns, with checkboxes reflecting current symlink state.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Symlinks' button or top-nav link opens the matrix view
Rows are confirmed skills grouped by source (source name as group header)
Columns are: 'Personal (~/.claude/skills/)' first, then each project in add-order
Each cell is a checkbox whose checked state reflects whether the symlink actually exists on disk (os.path.exists, not lexists)
Matrix refreshes from disk on every navigation to the view
Empty state message shown if no confirmed skills exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Symlink path: <target_skills_dir>/<skill.name>. Check with os.path.exists(symlink_path) — returns False for broken symlinks. Personal target is ~/.claude/skills/, auto-create if missing.

userStories.title: Symlink create and remove operations
userStories.description: As a user, I want checking a matrix cell to create a symlink and unchecking to remove it, with immediate filesystem effect.
acceptanceCriteria: Checking a cell: creates symlink <target_skills_dir>/<skill.name> -> <source_path>/<skill.rel_path>
Target skills directory is auto-created if missing
Unchecking a cell: removes the symlink with os.unlink()
If checking fails (e.g. permissions), an error toast is shown and checkbox reverts
If unchecking fails, an error toast is shown and checkbox reverts
No conflict handling yet (added in US-012)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use os.symlink(src, dst) for create. src is absolute path to skill folder. dst is absolute path in target skills dir. Catch OSError for error toasts.

userStories.title: Git update with output display and broken symlink scan
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pull the latest changes for a remote source and see the output, then be alerted to any broken symlinks.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Update' button on source detail panel runs `git pull` in the repo directory
Top-level 'Update All' button runs git pull on all remote sources sequentially
Pull stdout/stderr is shown inline in the detail panel during and after the operation
After pull completes, app scans all managed symlinks: any symlink where os.path.exists() is False but os.path.lexists() is True is broken
If broken symlinks found: warning dialog lists them with 'Remove' and 'Re-map' actions per item
'Remove' deletes the symlink; 'Re-map' opens a skill picker to reassign that target slot
If no broken symlinks: success toast shown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Broken = os.path.lexists(p) is True AND os.path.exists(p) is False. Store last_updated as ISO timestamp after successful pull.

userStories.title: Symlink conflict detection and resolution
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be warned when two sources have skills with the same name targeting the same destination, and to choose which source wins.
acceptanceCriteria: When two confirmed skills share the same folder name, both rows show a conflict indicator (amber icon)
Attempting to check a cell when another source's skill of the same name is already symlinked there shows a conflict dialog
Conflict dialog names both sources and asks which should win for that target
Choosing a winner: removes old symlink, creates new one, persists resolution in config
Each target column resolves conflicts independently
Resolved conflicts shown in config as ConflictResolution entries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Conflict = two Skill objects with same .name across different Sources. Check at checkbox-click time by scanning target dir for existing symlink pointing to a different source.

userStories.title: Missing project path handling
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be warned on launch when a registered project no longer exists on disk so I can fix or remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: On app launch, all registered project paths are validated with Path.exists()
Projects with missing paths show a warning icon (amber) in the sidebar
Their detail panel shows the missing path and two action buttons: 'Update Path' and 'Remove Project'
'Update Path' opens a path input; saving re-validates and updates config if valid
'Remove Project' removes the project from config after confirmation
Symlink matrix columns for missing projects are greyed out with a tooltip 'Project path not found'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Validate on startup in main.py before rendering. Store validation result in runtime state (not persisted). Re-validate when user updates the path.

userStories.title: Remove skill source with symlink cleanup
userStories.description: As a user, I want to remove a skill source and have all its symlinks cleaned up so I don't have orphaned links.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Remove' button on source detail panel opens a confirmation dialog
Dialog lists all active symlinks that will be deleted (symlink path per target)
On confirm: all symlinks from that source are deleted across all targets
Source is removed from AppConfig and saved
Cloned remote repos are deleted from ~/.local/share/skillmanager/repos/<slug>/
Local path sources are only de-registered (their folder is not touched)
Sidebar updates immediately after removal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shutil.rmtree() for remote repo cleanup. For each skill in source.skills, check each target's skills_dir for a symlink pointing into this source and unlink it.
